title: At the market
setting: You're at the bustling Mercado Oriental. A vendor calls out as you pass her fruit stand.

steps.speaker: Vendedora
steps.en: Hello! Come, come. What will you take?

options.feedback: Great opener. Asking the price first is exactly how you start.
Clear enough, though naming what you want gets you served faster.
A totally valid thing to say! Perfectly polite to browse.

steps.speaker: Vendedora
steps.es: Las naranjas están a cinco por veinte córdobas.
steps.en: The oranges are five for twenty córdobas.

options.feedback: 'Deme' (give me) is the natural way to order at a stand. Clean.
Also perfect — clear quantity and item.
Yes to what? She needs to know how many you want.

steps.speaker: Vendedora
steps.es: ¿Algo más, mi amor? Tengo mangos bien dulces.
steps.en: Anything else, dear? I have really sweet mangos.

options.feedback: 'Así está bien' is a smooth way to say 'that's enough.' Very natural.
Gets the point across, but feels a little blunt. A 'gracias' softens it.
Why not! Clear and confident. The mangos really are good there.

steps.speaker: Vendedora
steps.es: Son veinte córdobas. Gracias, mi amor.
steps.en: That's twenty córdobas. Thank you, dear.

options.es: Aquí tiene. Que tenga buen día.
options.en: Here you go. Have a good day.
options.feedback: Warm and complete. A good-day wish lands well in the market.
Lovely touch — 'gracias a usted' returns the warmth politely.
Twenty córdobas for fruit is fair — and grumbling sours a friendly exchange.
